author: @Hosanna_intheHighest

title: What I wish someone told me before I started my MSW Program. ** 4 week update** Fordham GSS
body:
I’m in the middle of my second semester of MSW program and I feel alone. I definitely needed to watch or talk to someone who is going through the same journey. I knew this video is old but it’s helpful.
I know no one on a personal level who is doing this journey. Everything you said is how I’m feeling. Many all-nighters aided in my 4.0 last semester. My advanced track MSW program is 18 months. I have 3 more semesters to go. As a mom of three, in a serious relationship, & full time employee, this is a lot! I have one of my kids graduating from 8th grade in a few months. Me and my oldest son is expected to graduate around the same time next year.
I don’t want to see another classroom or step foot in a college setting after this. But I guess I have to for my kids. 😅 someone told me good luck after I said I was in school for my MSW. They said it was because many students don’t make it and/or their mental health is in jeopardy as a result of the amount of work that goes into this. I can see some of my classmates falling apart. Thanks for this video. I feel less alone now
author: @octaviaeddings3765

title: What I wish someone told me before I started my MSW Program. ** 4 week update** Fordham GSS
body:
Hey,

I know this is a old video but I just started my MSW at Keuka College fully online w a full time work schedule and it’s hard lol It’s a lot of work and I’m in the 2 year program so I could only imagine how hard it was for you! I’m pushing through tho and trying to remember to stay focus because I am the same with not knowing how to focus as much as I thought while reading & doing lessons lmfaooo it’s so bad… love the video tho
author: @daeshasbudgeting

title: What I wish someone told me before I started my MSW Program. ** 4 week update** Fordham GSS
body: Your channel is a godsend! I'm so happy I clicked on your video. I'm completing my B.S. in Social Sciences (which is SW-adjacent) while concurrently enrolled in a Master's Track certificate program while working f/t. It's... A LOT. The only thing getting me through this is the fact that I absolutely LOVE what I'm learning and doing. Also, I'm so glad to hear that I'm not the only person who is interested in community activism-engagement and public policy! We need people at all levels!!
author: @kiak.matthews76

title: What I wish someone told me before I started my MSW Program. ** 4 week update** Fordham GSS
body: Great vid! Love the honesty. I finished my MSW at Western Carolina last year and had all these same thoughts while in the program. Literally 95% of my cohort was interested in clinical SW futures. My undergrad professors only talked about macro SW and never really touched base on clinical, so I didn't even know that MSWs could become therapists. That said, I did end up following the crowd and obtained my clinical license and am super glad I did because I actually do love it (I work in a psych hospital).
author: @Tazman2287

title: What I wish someone told me before I started my MSW Program. ** 4 week update** Fordham GSS

I think I'm gonna have to leave my FULLTIME AND get a PT job instead. But WE GOT THIS

title: day in a life of an Indian student in USA 🇺🇸
body: I’m Indian and it makes me feel disappointed in our Indian people because when they see someone doing better than them in life, they get jealous, envious and negative. They start making fun of that person. Gargipawar, you’re a good person. Don’t take these losers seriously. They are upset that you are in USA and they don’t have the same opportunity as you do. Ignore them! Do well in life regardless of these negative people and their hate and you keep progressing forward in all areas of your life. Make your parents proud. Good luck! ❤

title: COLLEGE MOVE IN DAY VLOG AT FORDHAM UNIVERSITY | dear adulting
body: Erica actually was quite meticulous about the whole thing. When I moved away for college I took so much with me and even in my pg I kept buying stuff and piling in my room and when covid struck I had all of my stuff there and I was stuck at my home and bringing them after was a herculean task, I still have so much left that I have stuffed away in my Grandparents and my Aunt's place and it has been 2 years already since I graduated. So a tip from someone who has been there done that and learnt the lesson hard way, always pack meticulously. Also, Congratulations Erica and all the best for the new chapter of your life. College days are the best. ❣️
